concept: Top-down action-adventure with tight real-time combat and puzzle dungeons. Core loop: explore overworld → enter dungeon → find the dungeon item → solve switch/torch/block puzzles → beat boss → unlock new areas. Combat: eight-direction movement, sword slashes with a chargeable Spin Attack, and a front-facing shield that auto-blocks basic projectiles. Progression is item-gated: Small Keys/Big Key open routes; Pegasus Boots add a dash for breaking obstacles and long gaps; Hookshot grapples across pits and stuns; Hammer flips/opens peg paths; Power Glove/Titan’s Mitt lift heavier blocks; Zora’s Flippers enable swimming; Bow, Bombs, and Boomerang provide simple ranged/control options. A Magic Meter powers select tools (rods/cape/canes) but is secondary. World switching between Light and Dark realms (via Mirror/Moon Pearl) reconfigures layouts and access. Enemy design teaches counters: Soldiers (melee/archer) for timing and shield angles; Beamos punish standing still in their sight lines; Wizzrobes phase in for brief attack windows; Wallmasters grab to reset progress; Hardhat Beetles shove you toward pits. Bosses are straightforward item checks that reward positioning and clean weapon swaps.
name: The Legend of Zelda: A Link to the Past
